tool_args.label: Queens Bus Network Redesign
tool_args.raw_text: Queens Bus Network Redesign Proposed Final Plan Addendum

payload.description: The Queens Bus Network Redesign aims to create a more efficient, reliable, and accessible bus system that better serves the needs of all riders, while prioritizing equity to address disparities in access and opportunity.

MTA Equity Framework and Title VI

payload.claim_text: Recognizing the presence of historically disadvantaged populations helps ensure that resources are invested, either through allocation or reallocation, and protected within these communities to reduce obstacles to transit access.
payload.statement: MTA's equity framework uses Title VI status and broader socioeconomic factors: transit dependency, minority representation, income, access to opportunity, and mobility needs.

tool_args.label: Equity Tier definitions

payload.claim_text: Tier 1: Significant Equity Consideration represents areas where transit-dependent populations, historically underserved communities, and/or those facing systemic inequities are most concentrated. Tier 2: Moderate Equity Consideration includes areas with notable, though less severe, equity challenges. Tier 3: Low Equity Consideration encompasses areas with comparatively lower equity-related concerns.

tool_args.label: Equity Score Index

payload.claim_text: A detailed Equity Score Index was developed using the MTA equity framework indicators. This index assigned Equity Scores to geographies, facilitating an evaluation of differences between the existing and proposed Queens bus network. Equity Scores were then categorized into three distinct tiers.
